_description: ESPN team/player stats for Brazil (3 group + R32) and Norway (3 group + R32), R16 BRA vs NOR 2026-07-05

matches.note: 8 offsides is a clear outlier vs the rest of their record

notes: Won Group C (7pts), GD+6, only conceded once in 3 group games. Vinícius scored in 3/4 games (1,1,2,0 -- blanked only in R32 vs Japan, their toughest opponent to date). Cunha now first-choice CF with Raphinha out injured since GD3 (2,1 goals in his 2 starts since, 3 goals total).

KEY BEHAVIORAL PATTERN (from prior postmortem): Brazil does NOT keep scoring once ahead -- zero 2nd-half goals across both group wins where they led. Do not inflate 'Brazil scores both halves'-type props.

KEY BEHAVIORAL PATTERN: the trailing/losing opponent's own stats are NOT suppressed by a Brazil blowout -- Haiti (down 0-3) still put up 4 offsides + real 2H SOT; Scotland (losing 0-3) still out-cornered nobody but had comparable corners. Do not assume Norway's own attacking numbers collapse just because Brazil is favored.

WORST-EVER RESULT PRECEDENT: BRA-MAR Q8 'Will Brazil win?' was gut-overridden to 1.00 (researched estimate was 0.58-0.60); Brazil drew; -51.97 RBP. Never submit outside 0.05-0.95, and never override a researched market-consensus number with conviction.
2W-1L group record, 6pts, GD+1. Beat their R32 opponent (Ivory Coast) 2-1, confirming a real knockout-stage scalp.

KEY LESSON: don't anchor Norway's ceiling to their weakest opponent (Iraq). Their SOT was actually HIGHER (7) against the tougher Senegal test than against weak Iraq (5) -- the same 'regime coverage' issue flagged in the Canada postmortem, just showing up as underconfidence risk instead of overconfidence this time.

Haaland did NOT play at all vs France (starter=false, 0 in every stat) -- full rest/rotation, not injury or poor form. He has scored in literally every match he has featured in this tournament (Iraq 2G, Senegal 2G, Ivory Coast R32 1G) -- 5 goals in 3 appearances, including a knockout-stakes goal vs Ivory Coast.

Ødegaard: 0 goals in every game but an assist in all 3 games he played (100% rate) -- pure creator. However, none of those 3 assists came against opposition anywhere near Brazil's quality (Iraq, Senegal, Ivory Coast) -- untested against elite opposition, same caution as the France-Paraguay SOT lesson.
